mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings revisions downward, causing equity markets to price in a near-term recession and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and business investment, which compresses forward revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both proxies decline concurrently, or joint signal IC fails to exceed 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation indicates supply chain normalization and improved capacity utilization, which expands corporate margins and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains flat or declines
failure_condition: SPX declines when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL stabilizes, or the interaction term shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, borrowing costs suppress equity risk premiums and force portfolio rebalancing away from equities, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite rates above 3.5%, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when PAYEMS shows consistent growth, or the rolling IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising inflation and slowing retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, where margin compression and reduced volume simultaneously hurt earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX rallies when inflation rises and retail sales fall, or the interaction signal yields a negative IC in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production coupled with rising real estate permits signals capacity expansion and infrastructure investment, which boosts forward earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Equity markets decline when both industrial production and housing starts rise, or the joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of instances.
